text.title: MCP Server Catalog Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the mcp-servers methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every catalog entry MUST include: category, install command, env vars, tool-count, last-release date, security score (0-3 on gitleaks/scope/maintenance), recommendation (use / monitor / avoid).
rule.rationale: Without scorecard, build-vs-buy decisions are vibes-based; scorecard surfaces risk and freshness at decision time.

rule.statement: Each entry MUST be tagged with one primary category (marketing, analytics, project-mgmt, dev, design, data, comms, security, finance, other). Multi-category tags are forbidden.
rule.rationale: One primary category keeps the catalog skimmable; multi-tag inflation defeats the purpose.

rule.statement: Servers whose last release is > 90 days old MUST be flagged `stale`. Servers > 180 days OR archived are marked `avoid`.
rule.rationale: MCP SDK evolves fast; stale servers break on SDK upgrades.

rule.statement: Servers requiring write-all or super-admin scope MUST be flagged `avoid` unless a read-only alternative is documented.
rule.rationale: Over-broad scopes amplify blast radius on any tool misfire; minimum scope is the policy default.

rule.statement: Catalog MUST be refreshed quarterly: re-scan release dates, security advisories, tool-count drift. Reviewer name logged.
rule.rationale: Without cadence, catalog rots; quarterly is the minimum to stay relevant.
